title: Implement Search and Bash tools
purpose: Implement the unified Search tool (combining ripgrep content search, file finding via glob, and fuzzy matching) and the Bash tool (streaming output with progress detection, risk classification integration from N-006 R8).
task: Implement SearchTool combining content search (ripgrep/grep), file finding (glob patterns), and fuzzy name matching. Implement BashTool that executes shell commands with streaming output, progress detection, and risk tier classification. Both implement the Tool trait from N-006. Testing: Layer 1 unit tests with real temp directories.

requirements.title: Implement Search tool — content search
requirements.spec: Search tool SHALL support content search via regex patterns across file trees. Results SHALL include file path, line number, and matching line content.
acceptance: SearchTool implements Tool with name 'search', effect ReadOnly
Input schema: { pattern: String, path: Option<String>, glob: Option<String>, max_results: Option<u32> }
Regex pattern search returns file:line:content format
glob filter restricts search to matching files
max_results caps output (default 50)
Test: create temp dir with 3 files, search for known pattern, verify correct file:line results

requirements.title: Implement Search tool — file finding
requirements.spec: Search tool SHALL support file finding via glob patterns, returning matching file paths.
acceptance: Input with glob but no pattern returns file paths matching the glob
Glob matching uses standard glob syntax (**, *, ?)
Test: create temp dir tree, glob for '**/*.rs', verify correct paths returned

requirements.title: Implement Bash tool with streaming output
requirements.spec: Bash tool SHALL execute shell commands and return combined stdout/stderr output. Long-running commands SHALL stream output with progress detection (output lines as they arrive).
acceptance: BashTool implements Tool with name 'bash', effect Process
Input schema: { command: String, timeout: Option<u64>, working_dir: Option<String> }
Executes via tokio::process::Command with sh -c
Returns exit code, stdout, stderr in ToolOutput
Timeout kills the process after the specified duration (default 120s)
Test: run 'echo hello', verify stdout contains 'hello' and exit code 0

requirements.title: Bash risk classification integration
requirements.spec: Bash tool pre-validate SHALL classify the command using classify_risk from N-006 R8. The risk tier SHALL be included in the ToolOutput metadata.
acceptance: Pre-validate calls classify_risk on the command string
Risk tier is included in tool output as metadata
Critical-tier commands produce a pre-validate warning (not block — blocking is orchestrator policy)
Test: 'rm -rf /' classifies as Critical, 'ls' as Harmless

requirements.title: Add search dependencies
requirements.spec: Cargo.toml SHALL add grep/regex and glob crates as needed.
acceptance: Cargo.toml includes regex and glob (or equivalent)
cargo check -p norn succeeds

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement AST/structural search (ast-grep) — deferred to later brief
SHALL NOT implement vector/semantic search — deferred
SHALL NOT implement fuzzy file matching (nucleo) — deferred to later brief
SHALL NOT implement bash command approval/permission system — that's orchestrator policy
SHALL NOT implement bash sandboxing — that's deployment configuration
verification: cargo test -p norn — search and bash tests pass with real temp dirs
Search returns correct results for content and file finding
Bash executes commands and captures output
